## Acceptance Criteria

Fail. `jit rdeps <id>` is documented and normalized as a top-level alias for `jit graph rdeps <id>`, but it no longer behaves like the canonical command by default.

## Architecture and Separation

No new storage/file I/O boundary violation found in the reviewed change surface.

## Functional Paradigm

No blocking functional-style issue found.

## Correctness and Invariants

Blocking issue below: alias semantics diverge from canonical command semantics.

## Safety

No unsafe-code concern found.

## Testing

Used the provided passed `cargo-ci` gate as primary evidence and did not rerun cargo tests. I did run `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; it passed.

Coverage gap: existing `first_guess_residuals_test.rs` only checks a one-level reverse dependency, so it does not catch the alias/default-depth divergence.

## Error Handling

No blocking error-handling issue found.

## CLI Contract

Blocking issue below affects the CLI contract for the new top-level `rdeps` first guess.

## Documentation

Docs state `jit rdeps` routes to `jit graph rdeps`, but current defaults make that inaccurate.

## Prior Review Feedback

`run_history` is empty, so there is no prior feedback to verify.

## Jit Issue Management

The listed dependency `527bf0a6` is `done`. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed.

## Findings

1. `jit rdeps` is not equivalent to `jit graph rdeps` after the later bounded-depth change. `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:160-162` defines the top-level `Commands::Rdeps` default as `--depth 0`, while canonical `GraphCommands::Rdeps` defaults to `--depth 1` at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:1477-1479`. `main.rs:1017-1019` then normalizes the already-parsed top-level value into `GraphCommands::Rdeps`, so `jit rdeps <id>` returns all transitive dependents by default while `jit graph rdeps <id>` returns only immediate dependents. This violates the issue criterion that the top-level reverse-dependency first guess resolve to the canonical command.

Total findings: 1

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
